rationale: Medicine L4 takes each L3 fellowship and decomposes into the named clinical entities, procedural categories, or drug classes practitioners self-identify with. Cardiology fellowships split by patient population + procedure type + device class. Oncology tumor-type leaves split by molecular driver + treatment modality. Surgery L3 leaves split by anatomical region or named procedure. I went 6-12 L4 per L3 where the field warrants it; some smaller L3s get 4-6.

uncovered_concerns: Several L3 leaves intentionally have no L4 children: stroke prevention is a single research community (anticoagulation + carotid + LDL), most surgical specialties at L4 just have anatomical splits, and the smaller fellowships (consultation-liaison psychiatry, addiction medicine, palliative care) live at L3 only — splitting them would create thin leaves.

Other L2 medicine specialties without L3 children (anesthesiology, pain medicine, radiology, pathology, lab medicine, PMR, sports medicine, etc.) similarly stay at L2 — they don't have fellowship-level depth that the L3+ classifier would learn well from sparse data.
